206856.explanation: In our city, we are working toward a new economy for a healthy planet. Our commitment is to innovative and clean industry. GroenLinks-PvdA is also clearly in favour of saying goodbye to the old fossil industry, creating sustainable employment.

206859.explanation: Of course seniors should have a say in municipal policy, just like other groups in society. Input can be given in multiple ways, such as citizens’ assemblies, where all groups are represented.

206861.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to give residents more direct influence on city administration – preferably in advance via a citizens’ assembly, not afterwards via a referendum.

206864.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA is working with ambition and great commitment to end the climate crisis and make Eindhoven sustainable. It is a considerable challenge, and we ask the same level of commitment from companies that establish themselves in the area. This is why we give preference to sustainable businesses.

206867.explanation: Like many residents, GroenLinks-PvdA is extremely fond of our four-legged friends. But dog ownership entails certain costs, which is why this tax cannot simply be done away with.

206868.explanation: Local businesses form the backbone of neighbourhoods. GroenLinks-PvdA therefore supports local businesses, so that they are not displaced by large multinationals. We want them to be able to decide on their own opening hours, and we remain open to dialogue if they want change.

206871.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to help people in need, whether they are single mothers or recognised refugees. If somebody has a residence permit, they deserve a good start in life, and cannot be left waiting in emergency shelters for years.

206872.explanation: Integration is investment. GroenLinks-PvdA believes that refugees, family migrants, labour migrants, and expats deserve a fair chance to participate from day one. Investing in language learning and facilitating pathways to volunteer or paid work will aid effective integration.

206873.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants humane support for people fleeing from war and violence. We believe that Eindhoven has a duty to make a significant contribution, especially while others are neglecting to respond.

206875.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports an inclusive society. A standard quiet room is an enormous investment. Where there is a need for prayer rooms in municipal buildings, of course we aim to make this a possibility.

206877.explanation: Residents living close to industrial estates have a right to clean and liveable surroundings. GroenLinks-PvdA wants fossil-based, high-polluting industry to leave both De Hurk and Eindhoven, and are willing to back up this position financially.

206879.explanation: Animals deserve to be treated with respect. GroenLinks-PvdA therefore favours the withdrawal of fishing rights. But we should be clear: many of the city’s fishing waters are not under municipal control.

206880.explanation: Thanks to the many people who make our nightlife happen, the night scene is more vibrant than it has ever been. As in previous years, GroenLinks-PvdA will provide opportunities for innovative nightlife initiatives and new pop-up and other venues – with due regard for local residents, of course.

206881.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA is proud of our diverse range of events, from carnival to Hit the City. We want to make it easier to organise neighbourhood activities and events, from small street parties to larger initiatives.

206887.explanation: In libraries, everybody is welcome. To read a book, to study, take a class, or simply enjoy a coffee. GroenLinks-PvdA therefore wants to make libraries free for everybody.

206889.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes that Eindhoven’s rich history deserves an important place. We have earmarked a lot of funding for this purpose in previous years, and plan to continue doing so.

206892.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants an inclusive society, where everybody can participate as fully as possible. Increasing inclusivity for BOAs will enrich and strengthen municipal enforcement.

206893.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants effective cooperation between police and BOAs. BOAs should not be taking over police duties, but instead act as familiar, trusted faces in the neighbourhood. The police are there for when things may get out of hand.

206894.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA prefers to invest in neighbourhood police officers who know the local area. Better a familiar face on the street than large-scale video surveillance.

206896.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants major investments in housing construction, with consideration for a wide range of ways to create quality, affordable homes.

206897.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to build more homes suitable for seniors in their own neighbourhoods. Priority and moving support will ensure mobility, freeing up homes for families and first-time buyers.

206899.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants major investments in housing construction. We believe that priority should be permissible, however the municipality will need adequate legal justification. In urgent cases, such as divorce or illness, priority should be an option in any case.

206901.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports housing in a liveable city. Parks, green streets, and the urban forest are extremely important. Building within the city boundaries will leave space for greenery.

206903.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA would rather invest in people, to support them and help them find work. Many welfare recipients would actually like to make a positive contribution to our city. With trust instead of distrust, we can prevent scandals such as the childcare benefits scandal.

206904.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants serious action to help people with debts. We would like to follow Arnhem’s example, by buying off debts and writing them off.

206910.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA intends to continue making the inner city as low-traffic as possible. We want the entire Kleine Berg, Emmasingel, Vestdijk, Stationsweg and Kruisstraat to be fully car-free, creating more room for open squares, cyclists, pedestrians, and greenery.

206912.explanation: The ring road is currently too dangerous for cyclists and pedestrians. GroenLinks-PvdA therefore wants to lower the speed limit to 50 km/h. This will also improve air quality.

206913.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants children to be able to play safely on the streets, and for the air to be cleaner. A speed limit of 30 km/h is sorely needed, as well as municipal enforcement.

206914.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to end the climate crisis by building sustainable, climate-proof homes that include water and vegetation areas for cooling. This is the best way to make homes cooler and to combat heat stress.

206915.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA greatly appreciates the informal carers in our city. We support them, and provide assistance where necessary.

206856.explanation: Our economy should be strong and also diversified (i.e. not too dependent on ASML). For this reason, we choose to invest more in the retention and expansion of our existing major companies, Philips and Thermo Fisher, and in the development of new tech startups.

206859.explanation: We would like to see periodic consultation between the municipality and seniors’ organisations on housing, healthcare, and the well-being of the elderly in the municipality.

206861.explanation: A referendum reduces complex issues to a simple yes or no. We see more promise in citizens’ assemblies, where residents enter into mutual dialogue and produce solutions with widespread support.

206864.explanation: Though we encourage sustainability, this is a step too far and is highly impracticable. We need a variety of industries in Eindhoven, but we do prioritise sustainable businesses when allocating land.

206867.explanation: We want to use the dog tax for well-maintained dog parks and sufficient bins.

206868.explanation: It is up to local businesses to decide whether to open in the evenings or on Sundays. General opening hours in Eindhoven have already been recently extended; we do not intend to extend them further.

206871.explanation: There are plenty of other groups who need social housing and have been on waiting lists for a long time, such as Eindhoven residents or key professions we need in the city, who should sometimes be given priority when allocating homes.

206872.explanation: We want to make it clear to all newcomers (labour migrants, refugees, and expats) ‘how we do things’ in Eindhoven, what their obligations and responsibilities are, and where to turn for questions or help. Our goal is participation and contribution right from the start.

206873.explanation: When asylum seekers arrive in the Netherlands, we should distribute them fairly across all municipalities. Once the national influx decreases (which we believe should happen), Eindhoven’s share will also decrease.

206875.explanation: We do not want to make this mandatory, that would be going too far.

206877.explanation: Relocation simply shifts the problem, and is not realistic in the short term. What companies should do is take measures to reduce both air and odour pollution, by investing in cleaner technologies. This will improve liveability in ‘t Ven.

206879.explanation: Casting  a line should be allowed, and remain so.

206880.explanation: We are in favour of a bustling nightlife, and support initiatives in this direction. Of course, immediate action is required for any disturbances.

206881.explanation: We want to be a vibrant and lively city, which should include festivals and parties, provided they are organised safely and respectfully and liveability is maintained.

206887.explanation: Libraries should remain accessible so that everybody – even those on a budget – can borrow books and receive help with learning or digital literacy. This way everyone can take part, whatever their income or background.

206889.explanation: Eindhoven does not have many monuments, which is why we need to be especially careful with those we have.

206892.explanation: We want BOAs to have a neutral presence. That is what the uniform is for. Obvious religious symbols do not befit the office of an authority figure.

206893.explanation: Enforcement officers should be properly trained and equipped with the proper tools, with bodycams and handcuffs at a minimum. If a baton is needed to ensure BOAs’ safety, we are willing to consider it under suitable conditions, such as for a designated group of officers.

206894.explanation: We are in favour of deploying more cameras and in a faster, more targeted fashion. We will expand the number of portable cameras, so they can be used for unsafe neighbourhood situations. Video surveillance should be expanded in the inner city, to avoid ‘blind spots’ and losing sight of criminals.

206896.explanation: We do not see this as a municipal duty. Developers and housing associations should ensure the construction of enough affordable homes.

206897.explanation: We want to encourage seniors to move to new, affordable homes, freeing up their larger homes for use by families with children.

206899.explanation: We believe that Eindhoven residents should get priority allocation for housing. This is where we feel at home. Specific agreements are already being drawn up with project developers, which will also apply to housing associations.

206901.explanation: We believe that ‘building in green areas’ is an unnecessary taboo, as well-integrated housing can go hand-in-hand with nature. When assessing housing developments, rather than looking at the quantity of green, we should be looking at the quality.

206903.explanation: We will take action against illegitimate benefit claims, and step up our enforcement efforts to align with those of other large cities. Recipients who make an error on their application will, of course, not be immediately docked.

206904.explanation: Proper support, early intervention, and mutual plan development work better than simply cancelling debts.

206910.explanation: There should be more space for greenery, squares, and parks in the city centre, however all destinations should remain accessible to residents and businesses by car. The ring road also needs to be optimised, to allow efficient handling of traffic.

206912.explanation: The section between John F. Kennedylaan and Eisenhowerlaan should stay at 70 km/h. The limit can be reduced on other parts of the ring road if road safety and traffic flow will benefit.

206913.explanation: This will improve local road safety. We have implemented this in many streets recently, and will continue to do so.

206914.explanation: We do not see this as a municipal responsibility.

206915.explanation: We offer a reduced parking rate for visiting family and friends, as well as for businesspeople, childcare and education staff, healthcare workers, and visitors to social and volunteer organisations. We plan to extend this ‘visitor scheme’ to provide additional hours.

206856.explanation: We consider it important for the Brainport region to continue to develop, which requires the arrival of innovative tech companies. We want an effective range of different companies in our region.

206859.explanation: Everybody counts, and seniors also have representation on the municipal council.

206861.explanation: The political parties represent the voices of Eindhoven. Through citizen participation, residents should be able to contribute ideas and have their say.

206864.explanation: All businesses are welcome, as long as they keep to laws and regulations.

206867.explanation: The VVD has been trying to abolish the dog tax for some time. Other pets are not taxed, and the revenue is not even spent on dog owners.

206868.explanation: The VVD supports freedom for businesses, provided they cause no disturbance to residents.

206871.explanation: Social rentals should be distributed fairly across all groups who need them.

206872.explanation: We need to invest the money spent on integration more wisely to help newcomers participate more fully.

206873.explanation: The VVD wants fewer asylum seekers to come to the Netherlands.

206875.explanation: Where possible, we should enable this possibility. We remain realistic, however: it will be impossible to fit out all public buildings with quiet spaces.

206877.explanation: De Hurk is the only estate for heavy industry in Eindhoven. It is financially impossible to move all of these businesses. They do need to adhere to legislation, however, and make investments to improve sustainability at an appropriate pace.

206879.explanation: Interference. Fishing with a licence is permitted in the Netherlands, and therefore also in Eindhoven.

206880.explanation: Vibrant nightlife befits a big city like Eindhoven. Where possible, nightlife establishments should be able to set their own opening hours.

206881.explanation: Everything is open to discussion in Eindhoven. Safety is paramount, however, as well as due consideration for all of our city’s residents.

206887.explanation: Asking a small contribution is reasonable.

206889.explanation: Monuments are important to the appearance of our city, but not at any cost.

206892.explanation: The VVD is against visible personal expression, such as religious symbols. Enforcement officers play a neutral role in our society.

206893.explanation: We support those who ensure our safety, and BOAs need to be able to defend themselves.

206894.explanation: Where necessary, we plan to place more cameras to combat crime.

206896.explanation: The VVD believes a first-time buyer loan is a useful means of helping people obtain their first home. But giving money away is unrealistic. These loans should be subject to an appropriately low interest rate.

206897.explanation: We support mobility among seniors, to free up larger houses for families.

206899.explanation: Eindhoven is growing, and we must remain open to newcomers from outside. We use waiting lists to determine who is next in line for housing.

206901.explanation: We want Eindhoven to be a pleasant city to live in. That means ensuring adequate green spaces.

206903.explanation: Welfare fraud is anti-social, and steals from people who genuinely need the support. That is why the VVD wants to tackle those who commit fraud.

206904.explanation:
Helping people in serious need is sometimes necessary.
 But the VVD believes it is even more important to prevent these problems in the first place.

206910.explanation: The inner city needs to remain accessible.

206912.explanation: Traffic flow is important. Safety at intersections must be improved, however.

206913.explanation: The VVD does not believe that 30km/h should be introduced in all residential areas. But in residential streets, safety takes precedence over speed.

206914.explanation: This is not one of the municipality’s responsibilities.

206915.explanation: The VVD wants these types of measures to remain practicable, and free parking for informal carers is extremely expensive to monitor.

206856.explanation: D66 supports progress. Attracting new tech companies is part of this strategy, so that Eindhoven remains the centre of innovation and technology, now and into the future.

206859.explanation: D66 believes that senior citizens should have a say in municipal policy. A separate seniors’ council is not necessary, however. D66 would prefer effective participation by seniors through existing channels, and will invest more where necessary to ensure equal treatment.

206861.explanation: D66 is in favour of democratic innovation and effective consultation opportunities for citizens. A corrective binding referendum is one potential example.

206864.explanation: D66 wants Eindhoven businesses to work sustainably and contribute to a clean city. At the same time, Eindhoven is a high-tech, enterprising city that must provide opportunities for people with ideas, boldness, and a drive to innovate. This way we can strengthen business, innovation, and sustainability in Eindhoven.

206867.explanation: D66 is in favour of abolishing the dog tax. The dog tax is not re-invested in dog-related facilities, and therefore has no policy justification.

206868.explanation: Local businesses need more flexibility and freedom. Retailers should be able to set their own opening hours, including in the evenings and on Sundays.

206871.explanation: Refugees granted residency in the Netherlands must be able to build up a normal life. That means a stable home. Stable accommodation is better both for them and for the city. D66 also wishes to continue investing in more rental social housing, so that nobody needs to be on a waiting list longer than necessary.

206872.explanation: D66 believes it is important for everybody who comes to Eindhoven to have the chance to be a part of the city and create a home. Investments in effective integration will help newcomers learn the language and facilitate their participation in society, improving outcomes both for them and for the city.

206873.explanation: D66 supports the Distribution Act, which means that every municipality should accept their allocated number of refugees. This way, Eindhoven can concentrate on proper accommodation and integration for its new arrivals, so that they quickly learn the language and can take part in society.

206875.explanation: D66 believes it is important for everyone to be able to freely practise their faith or beliefs. Public municipal buildings are neutral, and are there for everybody. D66 is therefore in favour of silent spaces. These rooms can also be used for prayer, but should not be designed specifically for a single religion.

206877.explanation: D66 wants a new, green, and vibrant neighbourhood in De Hurk. High-pollution industry has no place there. Only once these businesses have cleaned up their act should they be allowed to return to Eindhoven, at a different location.

206879.explanation: D66 is against a wholesale ban on recreational fishing. We do, however, want a greater focus on animal welfare and care for the environment.

206880.explanation: Eindhoven is a vibrant city. Nightlife is not only for relaxation, but also for social connection and talent development. The evening hours especially are when many musicians and performers take their first steps. That is why D66 wants to firmly anchor nightlife as part of the city’s culture.

206881.explanation: Festivals and parties contribute to a vibrant city. That is why it should be easier to organise them, day or night. This will also firmly anchor nightlife in the cultural life of the city.

206887.explanation: D66 believes that every Eindhoven resident should be able to borrow six books a year for free, without fines.  Ultimately, we would like for membership to be completely free for everybody in the city.

206889.explanation: D66 greatly values the city’s monuments. We ask private owners to take good care of them, and must also set a good example as a city.

206892.explanation: D66 believes that neutrality in enforcement is essential. Religious faith, however, is just as invalid a reason to doubt a person’s neutrality as their gender, sexual orientation, or skin colour.

206893.explanation: D66 is hesitant to grant weapons to BOAs. Violence is entrusted to police officers, who have been trained and authorised in its use. The range of duties for BOAs is expanding, however. If this trend continues and BOAs receive proper education and training, a baton may be an option in the future.

206894.explanation: D66 is not opposed to video surveillance, but it must be necessary and in proportion, with due regard to privacy. D66 would prefer more street police with an approachable and visible presence. That will create more contact, trust and safety, without infringing on privacy unnecessarily.

206896.explanation: D66 is in favour of a national rather than a municipal first-time-buyer fund, that would help starters in the market to purchase their first home. When starters sell their home, the money will flow back into the fund, maintaining the budget for future first-time buyers.

206897.explanation: When seniors downsize, their family homes become available for young families. For this reason, D66 is in favour of helping seniors who are willing to move, via financial support and guidance.

206899.explanation: The housing market is currently very difficult for young people. D66 believes they deserve a fair opportunity to live independently. Reserving 25% of new rental social housing will give young people the opportunity to start building a life, creating fairness in the housing market.

206901.explanation: D66 is committed to protecting our greenery: no construction in parks or nature areas. The only exceptions should be developments that genuinely contribute added value and are nature-inclusive, such as tiny homes or buildings that help enhance the existing vegetation.

206903.explanation: D66 always works based on trust. Currently, there are no indications of large-scale fraud. If that changes, we will re-examine the situation.

206904.explanation: D66 is open to freezing and writing off debts, as it will help people move forward, giving welfare recipients a sense of stability and a new start. Only where strictly necessary, and always with due care and effective support.

206910.explanation: Space in Eindhoven is in short supply. We therefore want to prioritise pedestrians and cyclists, with wide sidewalks, pleasant walking paths, and safe cycling routes. However, we do wish to ensure the city remains accessible to residents, businesses and people who do need to travel by car.

206912.explanation: D66 plans to invest in grade-separated crossings, such as tunnels and flyovers, and to lower speed limits to 50 km/h. This will make the ring road safer, allowing traffic to flow more smoothly and helping to build a safer and more accessible city.

206913.explanation: Everybody should be able to move around safely in Eindhoven. For this reason, speed limits in residential areas should be lowered to 30 km/h, and the streets modified accordingly. Research has shown that a maximum speed of 30 km/h is safer.

206914.explanation: D66 believes that people should be able to continue living at home, and technology can help. Sustainable cooling is becoming more important, but is not only relevant to people aged 75 and over. D66 therefore does not support a general subsidy scheme.

206915.explanation: Informal carers do important, often hard work. D66 believes they should encounter as few obstacles as possible. Free parking wherever they provide care helps in this regard.

206856.explanation: The SP wants a human-centred economy where cooperation, solidarity and participation take precedence, including for businesses. The municipality must not place blind trust in the Brainport growth driver, but invest in an economy that benefits ALL residents.

206859.explanation: The SP supports participation opportunities for all Eindhoven residents. Currently residents can speak up, but have no real influence on policy or implementation. We want change: a real say and genuine influence for all. Participation by seniors could also form part of a broader consultative body.

206861.explanation: A binding referendum could be a good way to give residents a decisive voice in policy, and ensure they are genuinely taken seriously.

206864.explanation: The SP wants business operations in Eindhoven to be as clean as possible, in the health interests of employees and surrounding residents. Keeping out businesses that do not do so is in line with this strategy.

206867.explanation: This is an old-fashioned and outdated tax.

206868.explanation: If all businesses could determine this for themselves, it would lead to unfair competition from large companies. We support SMEs, as we want to support and protect small businesses and their employees from excessive workloads.

206871.explanation: Recognised refugees currently qualify for urgent priority, and the SP would like to keep it that way. Urgency also applies to other victims, such as those of domestic violence, and single mothers with small children. Recognised refugees did not cause the housing crisis.

206872.explanation: The SP believes that integration is more than simply learning the language and getting to know our culture. Real integration means being able to work, having a roof over one’s head, and proper access to healthcare and education. Expats also have a right to integration, there are currently no schemes to that effect.

206873.explanation: Eindhoven is already welcoming more people than legally required, and is doing so effectively. We would like to keep it that way. Most newcomers are already enriching our society.

206875.explanation: Where there is a need, organisations should provide for this themselves. The town hall already has a prayer room. This is not the municipality’s responsibility to impose this onto organisations.

206877.explanation: Relocating these businesses within Eindhoven will not solve the problems, merely shift them to a new location where different residents will be impacted. We need genuine solutions to allow cleaner operations for polluting businesses throughout Eindhoven.

206879.explanation: The SP is neutral on this point.

206880.explanation: Current policy already allows extended opening times. Making it even easier to stay open will increase disturbance, and reduce opportunities to address it.

206881.explanation: The SP is in favour of organising festivals and parties for all Eindhoven residents. The permit process should include more consideration for local residents and nature, however.

206887.explanation: The SP strongly supports this idea.

206889.explanation: We are currently unaware that the budget for this kind of protection is too small.

206892.explanation: The Netherlands has a constitutional right to freedom of religion and the expression thereof. These expressions also promote mutual communication and understanding between the various religious communities.

206893.explanation: Only the police are authorised to use violence. They have a monopoly on it in the Netherlands, and have been thoroughly trained in its application. Security officers can monitor and observe, but violent interventions should remain a police responsibility.

206894.explanation: Eindhoven already has a lot of security cameras. While they do not prevent any crime, they do invade the privacy of our citizens. What does help are preventive measures – more of these should be implemented.

206896.explanation: The SP wants affordable homes for everybody, and would love 0% interest. Alas, we are not a bank. If this can be realised without the community bearing too much of the cost, then we are definitely in favour.

206897.explanation: The SP supports voluntary relocation where possible, and is willing to provide financial support where necessary.

206899.explanation: There is a major shortage of affordable rental housing in Eindhoven, in the social sector especially. To prevent homelessness and forced relocation outside of Eindhoven, they must receive priority.

206901.explanation: The SP is against building in nature areas, however building on former sports facilities (such as golf courses) should be allowed to combat the housing shortage.

206903.explanation: Fraud should be combated, and it already is. Research has shown that deliberate welfare fraud is rare, and that human error is sometimes labelled as fraud. We do not want to create another child-benefits scandal in Eindhoven. We should act from a basis of trust, not distrust.

206904.explanation: People wanting to escape their debts and make a new start deserve help, however we do not want this assistance to be abused in order to create new debt.

206910.explanation: The inner city should, of course, remain accessible to people who are dependent on their cars for access, and public transport needs significant improvements.

206912.explanation: Where there are benefits to safety, health, or the environment, then the speed limit should be made lower than 70 km/h.

206913.explanation: For liveability and safety in Eindhoven, a speed limit of 30 km/h should apply in ALL residential areas.

206914.explanation: Where necessary, the SP supports tailored facilities for people (WMO).

206915.explanation: Informal care should not be compromised by matters of affordability. This can be arranged through the existing carers’ support centre (Mantelzorgpunt).

206856.explanation: Having a variety of companies in Eindhoven makes us less vulnerable during economic downturns. Volt wants the municipality to strive for a diverse market of (tech) companies.

206859.explanation: It is useful to listen to different target groups. A senior council would provide advice on topics they raise themselves. It is then up to the City Council to weigh that advice.

206861.explanation: Volt wants residents to be truly heard via citizens' assemblies. A simple yes/no question cannot replace careful listening; it increases polarization and doesn't contribute to well-considered choices.

206864.explanation: Volt wants as many sustainable companies as possible. The municipality should be attractive to them and help existing ones transition. Legally, a municipality cannot simply dictate which companies are welcome.

206867.explanation: Pets, like people, are residents of the city. Facilities like dog parks cost money. It is logical to have a tax for this, provided the revenue is used to improve these facilities.

206868.explanation: Volt believes local entrepreneurs and business owners should have the freedom to manage their shops as they see fit. We have advocated for this for the past 4 years.

206871.explanation: People allowed to stay must have a chance to build a life. If we don't provide a roof, where will they go? We must also use existing buildings more cleverly to create more housing.

206872.explanation: Integration is vital, but you can't just throw money at it. Volt wants money spent wisely and believes employers should pay for the integration of labor migrants.

206873.explanation: We must at least meet the legal obligation. If more is possible (in terms of space or public support), it is always an option.

206875.explanation: Where possible, it is important to offer this to make Eindhoven an inclusive city for everyone.

206877.explanation: Relocating them won't help as they will cause nuisance elsewhere. We must enforce environmental laws more strictly and make industry cleaner at the source.

206879.explanation: Sport fishing is not good for biodiversity or water quality.

206880.explanation: Staggered closing times improve safety. If everyone leaves Stratumseind at the same time, it causes chaos. Spreading it out reduces pressure on public space.

206881.explanation: Volt wants a vibrant Eindhoven with events for all ages—loud and quiet, large and small.

206887.explanation: Expanding your world should never depend on your income. Volt wants to make the library accessible to everyone because knowledge and reading pleasure are a right for every resident, regardless of how well-off you are.

206889.explanation: Volt is emphatically in favor of protecting municipal heritage. The municipality of Eindhoven already puts a lot of money into monuments. Volt wants this money to be spent more effectively.

206892.explanation: By wearing a kippah, turban, or headscarf, BOAs are perceived as more approachable by a broad group of residents. Various countries in Europe, such as Sweden and the United Kingdom, have already proven this.

206893.explanation: In the Netherlands, who is allowed to use force in emergency situations is strictly regulated. Volt does not consider it necessary to expand this group in Eindhoven. We have the police for that.

206894.explanation: Volt is not by definition in favour of more camera surveillance. Only when safety outweighs the violation of privacy do we find the installation of new cameras justified.

206896.explanation: Starter loans mainly ensure that houses become even more expensive. Additionally, starters already have a reduced transfer tax.

206897.explanation: A relocation premium is not going to win anyone over. The costs of moving are not the barrier that stops elderly people from moving smaller. A premium would mainly be free money for people who were going to move anyway.

206899.explanation: Everyone who registers and actively responds should have a chance at social housing.

206901.explanation: Volt finds greenery important to keep the city liveable. Therefore, Volt chooses densification within the already built-up parts.

206903.explanation: This costs more money than it yields. Do you want your municipal taxes to go up because we have to hire extra officials for useless checks? Volt prefers to spend municipal money on things that actually benefit the Eindhoven resident.

206904.explanation: Buying up debt is cheaper than chasing people with debts and making them pay it off. In Arnhem, they are already working with a debt pardon, and that has ensured that many people who would normally go through expensive trajectories can now rebuild their lives.

206910.explanation: Car-free is important to give pedestrians and cyclists as much space as possible in the center. This makes the city more vibrant, liveable, and cozy.

206912.explanation: Volt wants the speed on the Ring Road in Eindhoven to go down to 50 km/h. This is safer and ensures a better flow of traffic. Additionally, it contributes to cleaner, healthier air.

206913.explanation: Our children must be able to play outside safely without danger. Therefore, the speed in every residential neighborhood must go to 30 kilometers per hour. That is safer for everyone and makes residential areas even more liveable.

206914.explanation: Hot summers cause dangerous heat stress for the vulnerable. Volt believes everyone should live in a healthy, cool environment. We prioritize natural cooling by planting more trees instead of air conditioning subsidies. One tree provides as much cooling power as ten air conditioning units.

206915.explanation: Volt believes that parking for caregivers should be free. Those who are there for others deserve support, not high parking costs.

206856.explanation: Economic growth should add value to society.

206901.explanation: The housing shortage is so severe that creative solutions are also needed, even if that means reducing green spaces.

206910.explanation: There are many elderly and disabled people who depend on cars for transportation.

206856.explanation: The region is too dependent on ASML. It would be sensible to introduce sustainable, future-proof businesses into the mix, but only once the region’s capacity can handle ASML.

206859.explanation: Advisory councils are welcome, and it is important for their voices to be heard. But seniors are a relatively large group of voters. Our priority is for minority groups to also have a loud voice. That is why we adopt a neutral stance.

206861.explanation: Of course residents should be able to give their opinions on important topics. A referendum should be a part of all important decisions, such as large-scale building projects or municipal reorganisations.

206864.explanation: Polluting companies are not welcome. We already have too many. Companies that belong in strong local economies but are not yet completely climate-neutral should be supported in their transition – local bakers, for example.

206867.explanation: The dog tax is spent on things that have nothing to do with dogs. We are therefore in favour of removing the dog tax, and as long as it remains, we want the revenue to be spent exclusively on dog-related facilities.

206868.explanation: Within Eindhoven’s borders, businesses should be free to determine their own opening hours. The Party for the Animals wants opening times to be part of permit allocation, with due regard for surrounding residents and limiting noise disturbance.

206871.explanation: Without housing priority, recognised refugees are forced to remain in asylum-seeker centres. This causes bottlenecks, pushing new asylum seekers into inhumane conditions. Recognised refugees have not had the opportunity to build up any time on waiting lists, and should therefore receive priority.

206872.explanation: Many newcomers will come to Eindhoven and will enrich our city, as we can learn from one another. Given the growth, we will need to make investments to ensure that all Eindhoven residents – old and new alike – can benefit.

206873.explanation: Refugees are welcome. The obligatory number is a minimum, and Eindhoven accommodates this amount with due consideration for local neighbourhoods. Given the difficulties elsewhere, we could certainly do a little extra.

206875.explanation: We acknowledge the value of silent rooms, offering peace and quiet for both religious and non-religious purposes. They do not necessarily need to be in every building, if there are nearby alternatives.

206877.explanation: Polluting companies that produce genuine benefits should become greener and more sustainable. Polluting industry should disappear, not be passed around.

206879.explanation: Naturally! The municipality has a ban on hunting land animals, which should be extended to include animals in the water.

206880.explanation: The Party for the Animals sees the importance of relaxation, however this relaxation should not compromise the health of surrounding residents and animals. The municipality therefore needs to establish a clear framework, within which nightlife establishments can operate freely.

206881.explanation: We are satisfied with the opportunities currently available. There needs to be a balance between the enjoyment of surrounding residents and animals, and that of the partygoers. Animals are extremely sensitive to noise pollution, which is why no parties or festivals are permitted in forests or parks.

206887.explanation: It is important to us that the cost of joining a library is not prohibitive to anyone.

206889.explanation: We should give municipal monuments proper protection. In our view, money is currently not the problem.

206892.explanation: Eindhoven follows the recommendations of the Netherlands Institute for Human Rights, which allow BOAs to wear a headscarf, for example. This allows a wider variety of people to become BOAs, reflecting a broader cross-section of society in local government.

206893.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants fewer harsh (repressive) measures, and more prevention and youth support services. More dangerous weapons for BOAs do not belong in this strategy.

206894.explanation: Security cameras only give the illusion of security, and do nothing to address the source of the risk. The Party for the Animals is hesitant about video surveillance due to the infringement on privacy. Cameras should only be used temporarily and subject to strict conditions.

206896.explanation: Municipal first-time buyer loans only postpone the problem, and serve to drive up prices. We would rather put this money towards building more affordable housing for this group.

206897.explanation: Relocation grants are not an effective instrument. Seniors’ housing needs to be built specifically. Senior co-housing communities (knarrenhofjes) are very popular – relocation grant or no – but there are none.

206899.explanation: Everybody has the right to an affordable home, regardless of where they come from.

206901.explanation: Absolutely not. There is still room to build inside the city. We must become more future-proof, with nature-inclusive homes that can withstand weather extremes, such as heat waves. Green surroundings are important in this respect.

206903.explanation: We work from a basis of trust. Investing more in combating fraud will hardly be productive. We would rather invest in strategies to help people break the welfare cycle.

206904.explanation: Debt cancellation is an important means to help benefit recipients with major financial problems.

206910.explanation: A car-free city centre is much more pleasant.

206912.explanation: The ring road should become a 50km/h zone, improving both road safety and air quality.

206913.explanation: A 30km/h limit in residential areas will result in fewer injuries, less emissions, less noise, and more safety. A lower maximum speed also creates more room for play. We therefore support more ‘living streets,’ where traffic is limited to walking pace.

206914.explanation: We support investments in natural ways to combat heat stress, such as trees and water.

206915.explanation: Informal care is important, but a parking allowance is not the way to reward it.

206856.explanation: Additional financial or other support for this is unnecessary. The municipality should provide businesses with adequate support if asked, however.

206859.explanation: This is why council members are elected, and LPF believes they should always be the ones accountable.

206861.explanation: This is why council members are elected, and LPF believes they should always be the ones accountable.

206864.explanation: This wording is too black-and-white. What about a travel agency that plans to sell holiday flights?

206867.explanation: Collection costs are too high, while enforcement leaves much to be desired.

206868.explanation: Keeping the city liveable will require clear agreements on exactly when certain businesses are allowed to open.

206871.explanation: As a municipality, we cannot disadvantage our own residents. If we do, it will do severe damage to the acceptance of genuine refugees.

206872.explanation: No, we do not need more money, what we need is clear agreements with them about what Dutch society can expect from them.

206873.explanation: Absolutely not! Eindhoven, in cooperation with other municipalities via the Association of Netherlands Municipalities (VNG), needs to convince the national government to stop the flow of asylum seekers.

206875.explanation: People who work eight hours a day have 16 hours to do this at home. Separation of church and state.

206877.explanation: But let us first see where there is space for these businesses to set up in our city and region.

206879.explanation: The government should stay out of how people practise their hobbies.

206880.explanation: If the municipality wants to safeguard liveability and security in the city, there will need to be discussions on what is and is not permitted.

206881.explanation: If this means there will be less bureaucracy, then YES! Liveability and safety should not be affected, however.

206887.explanation: Not for everybody! Libraries are also expensive to run. Making things ‘free’ for everyone in the city means they simply pay for it in other ways. Membership should still remain affordable for people on low incomes, however, and we support the policy that current students and young people aged under 21 should not have to pay.

206892.explanation: A uniform exudes authority. No other head covering is appropriate.

206893.explanation: BOAs need to be able to defend themselves against violent citizens. Something is better than nothing.

206894.explanation: Maintaining safety and liveability will mean more security cameras.

206896.explanation: This is what banks are for. The municipality could, as it has done before, introduce a specific guarantee or subsidy scheme.

206897.explanation: If we want more family homes, this could be a nice solution under the right conditions.

206901.explanation: If this means that the surface area of the city will increase, then with due care this is acceptable. But no impact on city parks.

206903.explanation: Where this is necessary, yes always. It is socially irresponsible to allow this fraud to continue.

206904.explanation: Yes, provided it was not the debtor’s fault. But there must be targets and limitations. If someone is not a repeat offender, a settlement can help get them back on track. The municipality can also work with creditors to reduce the debt.

206910.explanation: Low-traffic means fewer cars, but the city must still remain accessible, with enough parking spaces.

206912.explanation: Traffic flow at 70 km/h is better than at 50 km/h.

206913.explanation: No, not more anti-car policy in our city.

206914.explanation: No, this is unnecessary age discrimination.

206915.explanation: We need to ensure that these volunteers can continue to do their work.

206856.explanation: A healthy business climate with room for new tech companies generates innovation, jobs and economic growth in the region. By investing in enterprise and less regulatory red tape, Eindhoven will remain attractive to companies and investments.

206859.explanation: Seniors deserve a genuine say, not an extra advisory council. Input should take place via referendums and direct engagement, so that everybody is heard equally and policy does not become mired in yet another discussion group.

206861.explanation: Important decisions about Eindhoven affect everybody, and should therefore be taken by the residents themselves. A referendum gives residents direct influence over decisions that shape their city and neighbourhood.

206864.explanation: This will chase companies away, costing jobs and damaging the local economy. Enterprise should remain free, without ideological hurdles or additional municipal bureaucracy.

206867.explanation: Dog ownership should not be a cash cow for the municipality, and is an unfair additional cost for dog owners. It would be better for that money to stay with the residents themselves, rather than disappearing in to the city’s coffers.

206868.explanation: Businesspeople themselves know best when to serve their customers. Fewer rules and more freedom will create more turnover, employment, and improve the business climate.

206871.explanation: Rental social housing is scarce, and should be reserved first and foremost for our own residents. Priority for recognised refugees puts the housing market under additional strain and is unfair to our own starters, families and seniors.

206872.explanation: Integration is first and foremost the responsibility of newcomers themselves, not the taxpayers. Municipal funds should go to essential services for all Eindhoven residents, not to more and more integration projects without any clear results.

206873.explanation: More asylum seekers create additional pressure on safety, housing, and facilities, while Eindhoven is already struggling in these respects. The municipality needs to stop accepting asylum seekers, and opt for stability, safety and space for our own residents.

206875.explanation: Public buildings are for everybody and should remain neutral, without religious provisions that not everybody will use. Faith is a personal matter and belongs in the home and in churches, mosques or synagogues, not town halls or libraries.

206877.explanation: Forced relocation of companies will chase jobs and businesses out of the city, weakening Eindhoven’s economy. The municipality needs to stop its ideological environmental policy and concentrate on employment, growth, and a robust local economy.

206879.explanation: Recreational fishing is an innocent hobby enjoyed by many Eindhoven residents, one that has a place within our public spaces. A ban is excessive, limits freedoms, and comes down too harshly on ordinary residents.

206880.explanation: Greater freedom for the hospitality industry will create a lively city and a stronger local economy. Effective enforcement will maintain safety, while residents and visitors can continue to enjoy the city’s nightlife.

206881.explanation: Fewer regulations and faster permits will allow for more life in the city and greater freedom for local initiatives. Festivals and parties bring residents together, strengthen local culture, and help businesses, clubs and societies move forward.

206887.explanation: Libraries are important and must remain accessible, but making it free for everybody is very costly, at the expense of other services.

206889.explanation: Municipal monuments tell Eindhoven’s history, and give our city character and identity. Investing in them will preserve them for future generations, instead of allowing them to vanish or fall into disrepair.

206892.explanation: Enforcement officers should have a single clear presentation, making it clear to everybody that they act on behalf of the government. Religious symbols in uniforms create division, and do not belong as part of neutral street enforcement.

206893.explanation: BOAs are there to provide supervision and order, not to take on police duties. One way to attain greater safety is by putting more police on the streets, not by arming municipal enforcement.

206894.explanation: Safety on the streets is important, but more security cameras are not the solution. Cameras can be useful in specific problem hotspots, but should not lead to permanent surveillance and invasion of privacy.

206896.explanation: A zero-percent first-time-buyer loan will drive up housing prices and primarily help vendors, not starters. The solution lies in more affordable homes and lower taxes, not new loans that increase the debt burden.

206897.explanation: Moving house is a personal choice, not a municipal responsibility. This money could be better spent on lowering municipal taxes for everyone.

206899.explanation: Eindhoven residents often wait years for an affordable home, and therefore deserve priority. This will make staying in the city feasible for people who have already built their lives here.

206901.explanation: Eindhoven residents have already been waiting too long for affordable homes. Construction should be prioritised, with due regard for the thoughtful integration of green spaces in new developments. Without any additional space for new housing, first-time buyers and families will be left behind, while the housing crisis only worsens.

206903.explanation: Welfare benefits are intended for people in real need, not to be abused. Cracking down on fraud will keep the system fair, ensuring that more remains for people who genuinely deserve support.

206904.explanation: Writing off debts does not address the root causes, and rewards irresponsible decisions, with hardworking Eindhoven residents footing the bill. Aid should focus on providing support and opportunities, not writing off debts with public funds.

206910.explanation: A low-traffic inner city will make Eindhoven less accessible for residents and businesses who are dependent on their car or van. Retail and hospitality will lose customers, and the city will become needlessly expensive and impractical for people who simply want to live, work, and shop.

206912.explanation: The planned reduction to 50km/h is an unnecessary punishment for drivers, and will mostly create frustration. A better investment would be in improving intersections, and smart solutions to allow traffic to flow smoothly and safely.

206913.explanation: A blanket 30km/h norm will lead to poor neighbourhood accessibility, and mainly increase frustration. Traffic safety is achieved through tailored solutions and thoughtful design, not by imposing an ideological speed-limit regime on the entire city.

206914.explanation: Air conditioning can help combat heat, but subsidies are not the right answer. Municipal funds should not go towards individual purchases, but instead contribute to lower taxes and solutions that benefit everybody.

206915.explanation: Informal carers do essential work for family and friends and deserve support, not extra costs. Free parking lowers barriers, and keeps care human-centred and close by.

206856.explanation: Economic growth is important in order to offer employment and opportunities to Eindhoven residents. New businesses are welcome, provided they contribute to fair work, proper employment conditions, and investments in training to ensure the whole city prospers.

206859.explanation: We believe that seniors should have an active voice in policy. With a seniors’ council, neighbourhood service centres, and strong support for informal carers, we can ensure their ideas directly impact municipal decisions.

206861.explanation: We prefer different modes of participation, such as resident panels, neighbourhood meetings, and the active engagement of experts with experience. This allows residents not only to give their opinions, but to make a genuine contribution, influencing topics and considerations prior to decision-making.

206864.explanation: Sustainability is important to us, but everybody should be able to participate in the economy. We want to encourage and support businesses in their sustainability journey, instead of rejecting them out of hand, to ensure that jobs and opportunities continue to grow in Eindhoven.

206867.explanation: We believe that taxes should be fair to residents. Eliminating the dog tax will give people on minimum and low incomes more financial flexibility, and ensure that pet ownership does not become unnecessarily costly.

206868.explanation: We believe that local businesspeople should be free to decide for themselves what works best for their business and their neighbourhood. This way they can cater more efficiently to their customers and grow their business, without needless municipal regulation.

206871.explanation: Recognised refugees are Eindhoven residents, and are entitled to a dignified start. Fast accommodation promotes integration, work, and education, and prevents long-term emergency shelter. We also wish to invest in more rental social housing, so that showing solidarity does not have to disadvantage others.

206872.explanation: We believe that newcomers should quickly become a part of the city. Additional investments in language, support, work, and neighbourhood activities will help them become independent more quickly, joining in with their local community and Eindhoven society.

206873.explanation: Eindhoven is stepping up and doing its part for the humane reception of asylum seekers. Additional numbers are possible, provided there is sufficient backing from the community and enough facilities and neighbourhood support, so that both newcomers and the current residents have what they need.

206875.explanation: We believe that public buildings should offer space for everyone. Silent rooms are spaces for contemplation and prayer, but also for people who need peace and quiet. This helps promote inclusion, respect, and well-being in our city.

206877.explanation: We believe a healthy living environment is important. While pollution does need to be addressed, we first need to help companies to become cleaner and more sustainable. Relocation is one possible option, where improvements are not possible and neighbourhoods experience ongoing impact.

206879.explanation: We value healthy living environments, as well as recreation opportunities. Our focus lies on protecting nature and water quality and creating clear regulations, to ensure that people and planet remain in balance without blanket prohibitions.

206880.explanation: We believe a lively city is important, as well as safety and neighbourhood peace and quiet. Our focus lies on effective agreements with businesses, residents and local enforcement, to enable a vibrant nightlife without extra disturbance or risks.

206881.explanation: We believe that culture and social contact bring people together. Enabling festivals and parties broadens opportunities for local initiatives and diverse communities, strengthening connection, creativity, and creating a lively city for everybody.

206887.explanation: We believe that everybody has the right to access reading, learning, and digital assistance. Making libraries free will give young people, families, and those on low incomes more opportunities to develop and participate in society.

206889.explanation: We believe that Eindhoven’s heritage is important to identity and unity. Additional funding for protection and maintenance will ensure that monuments are preserved and remain accessible to residents and for neighbourhood activities.

206892.explanation: We believe that uniforms are what make BOAs professional and recognisable. The wearing of a headscarf, kippah or turban does nothing to change that, but instead demonstrates that law enforcement reflects the diversity of Eindhoven and helps ensure that everyone feels welcome.

206893.explanation: Safety begins with trust and proximity. BOAs must be visible, approachable, and have a de-escalating presence. Working together with residents and focusing on neighbourhood-based prevention will improve safety without resorting to harsher measures.

206894.explanation: Security cameras can help to increase safety in locations with frequent disturbance. However, surveillance should always occur subject to clear privacy regulations and in combination with neighbourhood BOAs and prevention, to ensure that it remains human-centred.

206896.explanation: We believe that young people and first-time buyers should have more opportunities to acquire their own home. An interest-free loan can help those with limited savings to buy a home, and ensure that housing remains accessible to Eindhoven residents.

206897.explanation: We believe that seniors who downsize should be supported. With a relocation grant, we help people make a smooth transition, freeing up larger homes for families and first-time buyers and improving housing mobility in the city.

206899.explanation: We believe that Eindhoven residents – including young people, first-time buyers, families, and seniors – should have fair access to a home in their own city. Priority for locals means that people who live, work, or provide care here are not left behind.

206901.explanation: We believe that expansion is sometimes necessary to ensure affordable housing. All the greenery that can be retained, should be. We will build with care and ensure new parks and green wedges, so that neighbourhoods remain healthy and liveable for everybody.

206903.explanation: We believe that more funding to combat welfare fraud is not the right approach. We do support fair and transparent monitoring without discrimination. Our priority lies in support, trust, and help with employment and debt, so that problems can be avoided.

206904.explanation: We believe that people in debt need opportunities for a new start. Even with smaller debts, support from the municipality can help create financial stability, providing guidance toward a steady income and more control over spending.

206910.explanation: We want a liveable and accessible inner city. While public transport and cycling should be encouraged, cars should not become victims of policy. Many residents depend on their car for work, healthcare, and accessibility.

206912.explanation: The speed limit on the Eindhoven Rondweg should remain at 70 km/h.

206913.explanation: Road safety is of paramount importance, especially around schools and busy residential streets. Instead of a blanket regulation, we advocate for a discretionary approach to ensure accessibility for residents, businesses, and emergency services.

206914.explanation: Older residents should be protected from heat and health risks. Targeted cooling subsidies can help the people who need it most, such as the especially vulnerable or those who live in poorly insulated homes.

206915.explanation: People should be eligible for free parking at locations where they care for family or loved ones in Eindhoven.

206856.explanation: Brainport and ASML should be allowed to grow, but only if amenities such as housing, infrastructure, and liveability can keep pace.

206859.explanation: In addition to a youth council (jong040) and a social domain client council, a senior’s council is also needed.

206861.explanation: Participation and consultation: don't talk about residents – ask them.

206864.explanation: As a party, we support businesses large and small. 50PLUS is in favour of sustainability.

206867.explanation: Because the revenue goes into the general fund.

206868.explanation: As a party, we support businesses large and small. We are in favour of national guidelines for opening hours.

206871.explanation: Equal opportunities for housing allocation! No preferential treatment for recognised refugees.

206872.explanation: We wish to guard against unjustified expenses, so that future generations are not saddled with large debts.

206873.explanation: Eindhoven should keep to the same support criteria as other municipalities, to prevent excessive influx. Opportunists are not welcome.

206875.explanation: There are currently enough facilities available.

206877.explanation: Addressing high-polluting industry is on our policy platform. With economic power comes responsibilities. Economic growth must be accompanied by a pleasant living environment and sustainability.

206879.explanation: Sport and culture are also important for a healthy and connected society.

206880.explanation: The recently adopted policy was developed with extensive input from residents and local businesses.

206881.explanation: The recently adopted policy was developed with extensive input from residents and local businesses.

206887.explanation: We encourage social connectedness and fair treatment in our communities.

206889.explanation: Eindhoven must take good care of our monuments and protect them.

206893.explanation: Enforcement officers (BOAs) should receive more powers and better equipment.

206894.explanation: More security cameras, including the use of sound recording, with consideration for privacy.

206896.explanation: 50PLUS supports a first-time-buyer loan, provided it is financially feasible.

206897.explanation: Building for the elderly to encourage mobility is the solution for the young.

206899.explanation: Local mobility and affordable housing is important for young and old.

206901.explanation: Eindhoven should be able to grow, but not at the expense of nature.

206903.explanation: Address all forms of fraud, including welfare fraud.

206904.explanation: This will vary, depending on the situation and the cause. Nobody should fall through the cracks.

206910.explanation: We want a city centre that is safe and accessible to all. Cars remain an essential means of transport.

206912.explanation: Keep current speed limits to ensure smooth traffic flow and prevent inner-city cut-through traffic.

206913.explanation: In neighbourhoods where everyone can move around safely and comfortably.

206914.explanation: A €2,000 air-conditioning subsidy for people aged 75 and over with heart, lung, or other chronic conditions.

206915.explanation: Parking in Eindhoven should always be free for all carers, informal or otherwise.

206856.explanation: Eindhoven already has a reputation as a highly innovative city with lots of tech startups. We would like to keep things that way, and attract new companies to Eindhoven so they can drive innovation here.

206859.explanation: We believe an additional consultative body for this group is not necessary. Roughly half of eligible voters are aged over 50, and this is also the group most likely to turn out to elections.

206861.explanation: Referendums force politicians to stand by their decisions and present them clearly to the public. For every major municipal decision, we therefore wish to hold a proactive referendum to give citizens input on important matters at an early stage.

206864.explanation: We object to additional stifling regulations for businesses. Businesses need to be able to keep doing business without oppressive legislation. NSC Eindhoven is not against sustainability policy in and of itself, but wants to prevent businesses falling between the cracks because of it.

206867.explanation: Revenue from the dog tax often lands in the general fund, rather than being used specifically for dog facilities. It is also unfair given that there is no tax for owing other pets, such as cats, that can also cause nuisance or disturbance.

206868.explanation: Where possible, local businesses should be able to set their own opening hours, giving them as much freedom as possible to conduct business.

206871.explanation: Anybody in an untenable situation deserves the right to priority. However, our city’s housing crisis means we need to reduce the pressure on available housing.

206872.explanation: To maintain Eindhoven’s identity, we must fully commit to the integration of newcomers, via a comprehensive approach involving the residents of Eindhoven. We want to establish local networks that allow newcomers to integrate informally into their neighbourhoods.

206873.explanation: We are glad that Eindhoven is stepping up and receiving asylum seekers. However, we wish to restrict numbers to the legal requirement, so that other municipalities will be motivated to do the same. This will also help maintain public support for local accommodations.

206875.explanation: We greatly value the separation of church and state, and therefore wish to keep religious expression separate from government buildings.

206877.explanation: We are committed to a liveable city. It is therefore necessary for polluting companies to move to locations where they cause the fewest problems for residents.

206879.explanation: There is too little recreational fishing in municipal waters for it to be a major problem. We do not see the need to implement a ban.

206880.explanation: Absolutely! NSC believes that the current range of nightlife activities is far too limited. We want to make sure there is enough variety, especially for young people.

206881.explanation: We want the regulations to be as simple as possible, and to ensure that events only need to submit a full, new application the first time. This will keep paperwork manageable and the city attractive for events.

206887.explanation: Membership should be free for people on low incomes, but not for everybody:  memberships are already inexpensive, and it will only become more difficult for libraries to stay open without additional income.

206889.explanation: Though we care deeply for our municipal monuments, we currently do not see the need to allocate additional funds for protection. We want to spend public funds thoughtfully, and do not see this as efficient.

206892.explanation: The strength of our police and enforcement officers lies in their role as neutral outsiders in situations that are sometimes emotionally charged. We therefore wish to strictly maintain this neutrality, by not allowing agents to display any religious symbols.

206893.explanation: BOAs are there to support the police force, and when the situation calls for it, police officers should act accordingly.

206894.explanation: Most cameras are currently still situated in the city centre. We also want residents in other parts of the city to feel safe. It is therefore important to place cameras at busy locations in the rest of the city, such as shopping centres, bus stops, and busy roads.

206896.explanation: As a municipality, we aim to spend tax revenue wisely. In our view, loans of this type should come from private lenders. We will also accelerate housing development by scrapping unnecessary regulations, and maintain the affordability standard to ensure that cheaper homes are built.

206897.explanation: Currently seniors already receive priority for rental social housing in the seniors segment. Senior citizens are also relatively wealthy in general, making it unfair for taxpayers to offer a relocation grant to all senior citizens.

206899.explanation: At NSC Eindhoven, this is one of our key action points. Eindhoven’s growth is putting enormous pressure on the housing market. We want to ensure that people born and raised in Eindhoven can remain in the city, and are not forced away. Eindhoven must be a home as well as a city.

206901.explanation: The housing shortage is currently extreme, and we must make fair choices. Building homes must take precedence. However, we would prefer to build new homes with green spaces, so that residents do not just see concrete, but also have access to some fresh air.

206903.explanation: The NSC knows like no other what a harsh approach can do. We want to combat fraud, but focusing too much on this aspect will lead to a second ‘child-benefits scandal.’ Welfare fraud is also relatively rare, and focusing on it would be unproductive.

206904.explanation: The Netherlands has a debt industry that is not interested in helping people with debts, but just wants to make money. As municipalities we should therefore not write debts off, but take them on in order to work with the debtor on a human-centred solution to their problem.

206910.explanation: We believe the inner city should remain accessible, including for drivers. We therefore oppose the municipality’s traffic circulation plan, which aims to keep cars out of the city as much as possible.

206912.explanation: NSC Eindhoven wants to give drivers enough freedom to get around, while also keeping the city accessible and functional. Reducing speed will create unnecessary bottlenecks. We therefore oppose the municipality’s traffic circulation plan, which aims to keep cars out of the city.

206913.explanation: To keep our neighbourhoods safe, NSC Eindhoven is open to reducing speed limits to 30km/h where this is not already the case.

206914.explanation: Where there is a proven need for air conditioning due to health reasons, we believe the municipality should provide support by means of a subsidy. Where this is not the case, it is completely up to the owner.

206915.explanation: Absolutely! We aim to ensure that informal carers in Eindhoven receive enough support.

206859.explanation: Ouderen kunnen al meepraten. Wij hebben actieve ouderen op de lijst welke niet in de slachtofferrol zitten.

206861.explanation: Het volk dient gehoord te worden. Van de straat naar de raad.

206868.explanation: Binnen de normen en kaders moet vrij ondernemerschap mogelijk zijn. Bescherm de kleine ondernemer. Max. aantal uren per dag open.

206871.explanation: Zeer zeker niet! Het valt al jaren niet mee voor Eindhovense jongeren en personen in dienstverlenende beroepen om huisvesting te vinden.

206889.explanation: Niet alleen meer geld, ook echt meer bescherming.

206893.explanation: Daar zijn wij het mee eens echter zijn wij van mening dat de BOA's op serieuze onderwerpen dienen te handhaven en niet op een stoelpoot die 10cm te ver staat op een terras.

206896.explanation: Is dit een realistische vraag voor een gemeentelijke bank?

206897.explanation: Ouderen willen wel kleiner wonen maar dit wordt zelfs onmogelijk gemaakt.

206899.explanation: Wel voorrang voor werknemers zorg-, hulpverlenings en onderwijsberoepen.

206901.explanation: Eindhoven is doorgeslagen in dier en milieu. Mensen zijn minder belangrijk blijkbaar.

206904.explanation: Ligt aan de reden hoe zij er ingekomen zijn.

206910.explanation: Het blijkt dat dit voor het MKB en merendeel van de inwoners onwenselijk is.

206912.explanation: In elk geval niet lager. Zeker geen 30.

206913.explanation: Niet in alle wijken en gebieden. Kan wel indelen.

206915.explanation: Moet er wel een parkeerplaats zijn.

206856.explanation: PRO Eindhoven agrees, as innovative tech companies contribute to employment and economic development in the Brainport Eindhoven region. This growth must remain in balance with liveability, accessibility and the interests of residents, so that development enhances the city as a whole.

206859.explanation: This is in line with PRO Eindhoven’s core participation principle. Residents, including seniors, should be involved from the outset in policy that affects them.

206861.explanation: PRO Eindhoven supports referendums that actively involve residents at an early stage, and that serve as the democratic keystone of an open and well-considered participation process.

206864.explanation: PRO Eindhoven wishes to set sustainability as a criterion for new businesses. Companies wanting to settle here must be sustainable or clearly on the path to sustainability, backed by clear agreements and municipal support.

206867.explanation: This is an outdated tax without any immediate returns for the people; the revenue disappears into the general fund, while dog owners have additional administrative costs. PRO Eindhoven wants less bureaucracy and fair regulations that work for residents. This tax should therefore go.

206868.explanation: Businesses and residents themselves know what works. A local budget is in line with neighbourhood-centred and practical policy.

206871.explanation: PRO Eindhoven wants fairness and equal opportunities in housing allocation.

206872.explanation: According to our platform, investments in language, work and social participation are essential for robust neighbourhoods and cohesion.

206873.explanation: Our platform prioritises community support, capacity, and liveability, while focusing on our statutory duties.

206875.explanation: PRO Eindhoven wants inclusion and connection via tailored and practical solutions, but does not support a blanket requirement for quiet spaces in all public buildings.

206877.explanation: PRO Eindhoven disagrees, as not all polluting companies should automatically be forced to move. We favour a tailored approach: tackling nuisance and pollution through clear regulations, supervision, and sustainability measures, ensuring improvements in liveability and sustainability without unnecessary relocations.

206879.explanation: PRO Eindhoven wants a balance between recreation, animal welfare, and clear regulations.

206880.explanation: PRO Eindhoven agrees, because a vibrant nightlife contributes to economic development and a lively city. Opening times could be extended, if done so with care and clear agreements on safety, surveillance, and liveability, and with due consultation of residents.

206881.explanation: Culture and events strengthen social cohesion and make for a lively city, as stated in our platform.

206887.explanation: PRO Eindhoven agrees, as knowledge, information and development should be easily accessible to everybody. The library is already free for children; by making it free for all, we strengthen equal opportunities, education, and social participation for everyone.

206889.explanation: PRO Eindhoven agrees, as the protection of municipal monuments contributes to a liveable and future-proof city with a strong identity.

206892.explanation: These are already permitted in Eindhoven; PRO Eindhoven believes that BOAs should be neutral and professional, and that religious expression is no problem provided that safety, recognisability, and equal treatment are not compromised.

206893.explanation: PRO Eindhoven believes BOAs must be able to work safely, but supports the baton only if it clearly improves safety and subject to proper training, clear rules of engagement, and robust supervision.

206894.explanation: PRO Eindhoven supports permanent security cameras at busy and high-risk locations such as stations and nightlife areas, but not as a general blanket measure throughout the city.

206896.explanation: Supporting first-time buyers is a part of fair housing policy, provided it is financially responsible.

206897.explanation: PRO Eindhoven sees a relocation grant as costly and relatively ineffective. The impact is limited, and implementation is expensive. PRO Eindhoven therefore opts for long-term investments in homes for seniors, and improved mobility on the housing market.

206899.explanation: PRO Eindhoven believes it is reasonable for Eindhoven residents seeking housing to be prioritised as far as legally possible in the allocation of social housing, while preserving a fair and transparent system.

206901.explanation: PRO Eindhoven disagrees. New homes are necessary, but not at the expense of green surroundings. We favour thoughtful construction within the city and the judicious use of space, with retention of nature and liveability as important considerations in spatial decisions.

206903.explanation: PRO Eindhoven believes that the abuse of services should be addressed to ensure confidence in social security, with surveillance always going hand-in-hand with support and a human-centred approach.

206904.explanation: PRO Eindhoven agrees, as the purchase and cancellation of debts can give people facing serious financial difficulties a way forward, with initial support followed by supervision, aiming for a debt-free future.

206910.explanation: PRO Eindhoven supports a low-traffic city centre where this improves liveability and safety, but believes the centre should remain easily accessible for residents, businesses, emergency services, and people with disabilities.

206912.explanation: This will improve traffic flow, road safety, and liveability.

206913.explanation: PRO Eindhoven supports a 30 km/h limit in residential areas where evidence shows it contributes to traffic safety and liveability, but believes that implementation should be tailored to each street and neighbourhood.

206914.explanation: PRO Eindhoven disagrees. A general air-conditioning subsidy is not the answer: we prefer a tailored approach and targeted (preferably sustainable) support for older people who truly need it.

206915.explanation: PRO Eindhoven believes that informal carers deserve practical support, so they can provide care without additional obstacles, such as parking expenses.

206856.explanation: ASML gives our region a unique position in the world. Strengthening that position by attracting new tech companies is a wise choice not only for our generation, but for those to come.
