206859.explanation: In our view, seniors are already adequately represented on the Eindhoven municipal council.

206861.explanation: The council has a mandate from Eindhoven residents, and with it the responsibility to govern the city and make balanced judgements. A referendum can be guided too easily by pure emotion.

206864.explanation: We believe this proposition is far too simplistic. Who decides when a business is ‘sustainable’? All companies should adhere to the permits issued, subject to strict municipal enforcement, and closure if necessary.

206867.explanation: This is inevitable in the long term — it is the national trend. But the loss of revenue will ultimately have to be covered by people who do not own a dog. Compared to the total costs of owning a dog, the dog tax is a token amount.

206868.explanation: This proposition is far too broad; local businesses can already set their own opening and closing hours, but are often forced to stay open to compete with large national chains. We do see the dangers of the 24-hour economy, however, and the value of enough quiet time in society.

206871.explanation: The housing market is complex and constrained. We must act wisely, and tailored solutions are better than blanket measures. What we need most of all are lots – and lots – of new homes.

206872.explanation: ‘More money’ is rarely a solution. We must do what is right, and integration is certainly a part of that. Our priority is to teach newcomers the language as quickly as possible (English if necessary), and enable them to contribute to society through paid or volunteer work.

206873.explanation: Even if we only do our national part, we will still have more than enough problems to solve on the housing market.

206875.explanation: Peace and quiet is a scarce commodity nowadays. A silent room would be very welcome.

206877.explanation: But we want to go a step further: De Hurk should be rezoned as residential in its entirety. All industry must disappear. An added advantage is that the houseboats in Beatrixkanaal can stay where they are.

206879.explanation: For many, fishing is a calming and relaxing activity. We do not want to take this away.

206880.explanation: In our view, current regulations already offer plenty of options.

206881.explanation: In our view, current regulations already offer plenty of options.

206887.explanation: Many residents can easily afford a membership, and those who cannot should receive support.

206889.explanation: Our municipal monuments are already few in number, but we should cherish those we have, even if that means spending more.

206892.explanation: The Netherlands has the constitutional right to freedom of religion. Enforcement officers also need to be able to wear Christian religious symbols. One condition, however, is that these symbols do not prevent them from doing their job.

206893.explanation: BOAs must be enabled to do perform their duties properly, which may even require more than a baton.

206894.explanation: Not more cameras per se, but the opportunity to deploy more. We believe more enforcement presence would be more effective.

206896.explanation: The municipality is not a bank. We must ensure housing development.

206897.explanation: Seniors are quite willing to move, and in many cases can pay for it themselves. It is the municipality’s responsibility to ensure a broad range of adequate housing. That will encourage mobility automatically.

206899.explanation: We need to be extremely careful allocating priority, as someone else is always disadvantaged.

206901.explanation: We must not paint ourselves into a corner. Green areas that may need to go can be compensated for elsewhere. We must constantly strive for a balance – but build we must.

206903.explanation: Not more money, enough is being spent already. Far greater social evils include undermining and drug crime, where there is far more to be gained for society.

206904.explanation: This would definitely send the wrong message. People with problematic debts should receive quality, long-term support, which requires a tailored approach.

206910.explanation: We would prefer a completely car-free city, provided that parking garages remain accessible.

206912.explanation: 70 km/h, with phased traffic lights as much as possible. Ultimately, all intersections on the ring road should be grade-separated.

206913.explanation: 30km/h should be introduced (and enforced) where necessary, but not everywhere.

206914.explanation: Many people can afford this quite easily. For those who need it but cannot afford it, we must look at tailored solutions.

206915.explanation: This sounds like a good idea, but is not enforceable and should be avoided.

206856.explanation: EVE agrees here. Tech companies create prosperity and the jobs of the future, and Eindhoven plays a key role in their progress. At the same time, new companies’ growth should be socially responsible, so that economic development does not compromise liveability, affordability, or quality of life.

206859.explanation:
EVE believes that citizen participation is essential. Both seniors and younger generations should be properly engaged in policymaking.
 We facilitate citizen input through a range of participatory mechanisms.
 Council members are selected at random, ensuring equal opportunity and accurate representation.

206861.explanation: EVE supports resident input, but does not see referendums as the right instrument. They only offer a yes/no choice without room for nuance, dialogue, or joint consideration, and increases the risk of polarisation and narrowing the debate.

206864.explanation: EVE believes that sustainability is important, and expects companies to be committed to the climate and liveability. Strict admission requirements are not realistic, however. We prefer encouragement and realistic obligations with clear standards and enforcement, so that Eindhoven can improve sustainability without foregoing economic opportunities.

206867.explanation: EVE supports eliminating the dog tax. It is not a large source of income and mainly affects low-income earners, while dogs offer companionship to singles and seniors especially, helping to combat isolation.

206868.explanation: EVE believes it is important for businesses to do business, but complete freedom of opening hours creates unfair competition and puts additional strain on employees. Joint agreements ensure fair competition, proper working conditions, and liveable neighbourhoods.

206871.explanation: EVE believes that recognised refugees have the right to proper accommodation and occasionally priority, but not across the board and not only for social rentals. We favour a balanced approach with more homes, so that nobody is left out and the housing shortage is solved instead of merely transplanted.

206872.explanation: EVE believes that investments in the integration of newcomers is essential. Effective support for language, work, education, and community participation means they gain independence and can take part sooner, which is in the interests of both newcomers and the city.

206873.explanation: Accepting more than the legal requirement should not be a goal in itself. It depends on the circumstances at hand. It may increase in times of crisis; at other times the amount may vary. Quality, distribution, and solidarity are the important elements.

206875.explanation: EVE supports a clear separation of church and state. However, a silent space for anybody who needs it – including for non-religious purposes – could be a valuable addition.

206877.explanation: De Hurk is situated beside residential areas. EVE considers persistent nuisance and health risks unacceptable, but solutions must be carefully designed to avoid merely shifting the problem, to support sustainability where possible, and to keep workers and liveability in mind.

206879.explanation: EVE wonders whether we should have an opinion here. For many residents, fishing is a relaxing activity.

206880.explanation: EVE supports a vibrant nightlife and sees possibilities for extended opening hours, but only under clear conditions and with due regard for liveability, safety, and evening rest. Tailored solutions, effective enforcement, and clear agreements are essential.

206881.explanation: EVE believes that festivals and parties contribute to liveliness and community in Eindhoven, and should be easier to organise. The municipality should support initiators more proactively with guidance, clear procedures, and suitable locations, with attention to safety and liveability.

206887.explanation: EVE believes that library access should be free for all. Learning and culture should not be dependent on income. Free library access lowers barriers and contributes to education, participation, and equal opportunities, which justifies public spending.

206889.explanation: EVE believes that municipal monuments are an important part of Eindhoven’s identity and history. Investing in protection and maintenance preserves this heritage, allowing it to be used actively in the city and local areas for the long term.

206892.explanation: EVE believes that BOAs should have a neutral appearance. Religious symbols do not belong on public-service uniforms. This way, enforcement officers are recognisable and impartial to everyone, and can carry out their work with authority and in a way that brings people together.

206893.explanation: EVE believes that BOAs should be safe at work, but we do not know whether a baton is the solution. For this reason, we would like to conduct a pilot to ascertain the value of a baton.

206894.explanation: EVE greatly values safety. Cameras can be of help at specific locations, but should not be a blanket solution. Privacy and proportionality take precedence. Prevention, enforcement presence, and robust neighbourhoods remain essential, although video surveillance can increase public safety.

206896.explanation: EVE believes that first-time buyers with a low or medium income deserve support, but only selectively and in a supplementary manner. Generic loans drive up prices. The real solution lies in building enough affordable homes: the housing shortage needs to be solved, not transplanted.

206897.explanation: EVE wants to encourage mobility on the housing market. A relocation grant for seniors could help to free up larger homes for families. This grant should be targeted, temporary and issued through a municipal development company, provided there are enough suitable homes for older people.

206899.explanation: EVE believes that Eindhoven residents should have fair access to rental social housing in their own city. Giving priority to local residents when allocating housing helps strengthen their connection to the city and reduces local housing shortages, while following the law and protecting vulnerable groups.

206901.explanation: EVE believes that the housing shortage is serious, and more construction is needed. This could occur in current green zones, subject to strict conditions and compensation elsewhere. Green spaces remain essential. That is why we plan to focus on multi-functional building inside the city, and using space wisely.

206903.explanation: EVE believes that the municipality should operate on the basis of trust, not distrust. Less monitoring gives welfare recipients more opportunities to move on to work or education, and gives officials more time to provide support. This is a more effective, efficient, and humane approach.

206904.explanation: EVE believes that welfare recipients with problem debts deserve opportunities. Targeted debt buy-back and partial debt relief give people breathing space to get back out into the community and re-enter work or education, with proper support.

206910.explanation: EVE believes that a low-traffic city centre fosters liveability and safety. Less car traffic means cleaner air and more space for walking and cycling, provided it is implemented thoughtfully, gradually, and with proper access for residents, businesses, and people with disabilities.

206912.explanation: EVE believes that the speed limit on the Eindhoven ring road should remain at 70km/h. It strikes a good balance between traffic flow, safety, and liveability. Further speed reductions should only occur where clearly necessary, so that the ring road remains accessible.

206913.explanation: EVE believes that residential areas are for living in, and should not be treated as through-roads. A speed limit of 30km/h increases road safety, reduces noise pollution, and makes streets safer for children, seniors, and cyclists.

206914.explanation: EVE believes that protection against extreme heat is important, especially for vulnerable seniors. However, we do believe that financial support should go to those who want air conditioning but cannot afford it.

206915.explanation: EVE believes that informal carers are indispensable, and that they should be able to provide care without additional obstacles. Free parking on site can help, provided it is issued rigorously and on a case-by-case basis via an informal carer scheme, to prevent misuse.

statements.title: The Eindhoven city centre should be made as low-traffic as possible.
There should be a seniors’ council , to give senior citizens a say in municipal policy.
The municipality should actively encourage new tech companies to move to the Eindhoven Brainport region.
statements.moreInfo: Brainport Eindhoven is the region in and around Eindhoven containing many companies, government organisations, and research institutes that focus on technology and innovation.

statements.title: Refugees with residency permits should be prioritised for social rental housing.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.
Only businesses with sustainable practices should be allowed to set up in Eindhoven.
All municipal public buildings should include a room for silent prayer.
Municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) patrolling for safety in Eindhoven should carry a baton.
The municipality should take in more asylum seekers than the legal minimum.
Local businesses should be able to set their own opening hours.
Eindhoven should prohibit recreational fishing in municipal bodies of water.
statements.moreInfo: Municipal bodies of water are those managed by the municipality itself within its boundaries, and include ditches, canals, ponds, and the water in parks.

statements.title: Pollution-causing businesses on the De Hurk industrial estate should move to another location as soon as possible.
It should be easier to organise festivals and parties in Eindhoven.
Eindhoven should invest more in the protection of municipal monuments.
Eindhoven should spend more on integrating newcomers to society.
First-time home buyers should be eligible for a special municipal loan at 0% interest.
Eindhoven should have more video surveillance (security cameras).
The municipality should offer a relocation grant to senior citizens who move into smaller homes.
The speed limit on the Eindhoven Rondweg should remain at 70 km/h.
It should be easier for nightlife establishments to stay open late at night.
The municipality should take over the debts of welfare recipients experiencing major financial difficulty, and write them off.
It should be possible to expand city housing into green areas.
Municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) should be allowed to wear a headscarf, yarmulke, or turban.
The residential speed limit in Eindhoven should be lowered to 30 km/h.
Local residents should be given as much priority as possible in the allocation of social rental housing.
People should be eligible for free parking at locations where they care for family or loved ones in Eindhoven.
The municipality should invest more to combat  welfare fraud.
Eindhoven residents should be able to give their opinion on important matters via a referendum.
People aged 75 or over should be eligible for a municipal air-conditioning grant.
Libraries should be free for all Eindhoven residents.
